commit.message:
chore: pin inferno to c20787c (TX zombie fix)

Updates inferno dependency to include multicast TX flow cleanup:
- Hard send errors on activated multicast flows start a 10s timer
- After 10s, flow is marked expired and cleaned up by background task
- Prevents zombie TX flows from accumulating after subscriber disconnect

commit.message:
fix: default site theme to light mode

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Refactor color system to be config-aware and function-based

Status colors are now derived from workflow state_groups rather than
hardcoded status names, so any user-defined workflow gets correct
coloring automatically.

internal/ui/colors.go:
- Replace StatusColors (state→color map) with GroupColors (group→color)
  — only 'active' and 'completed' groups need colors; all others unstyled
- Add StatusStyleFunc(w *config.Workflow) func(string) lipgloss.Style
  which builds a state→group reverse index and returns a closure; used
  by renderTable call sites
- Add TypeStyleFunc() func(string) lipgloss.Style returning a closure
  for consistency with StatusStyleFunc; backed by existing TypeColors
- Keep TypeStyle(type, base) for direct use in pm tree's formatNode

cmd/pm/table.go:
- renderTable now accepts statusStyle/typeStyle as func(string) lipgloss.Style
  (nil = no coloring); styleFunc calls them directly — zero knowledge of
  colors, groups, or config inside renderTable itself

cmd/pm/list.go, search.go:
- Pass ui.StatusStyleFunc(workflow) and ui.TypeStyleFunc() to renderTable

cmd/pm/milestone.go:
- Pass nil, nil (no status/type columns to color)

cmd/pm/tree.go:
- Load workflow in Run; pass to renderTree → renderChildren → formatNode
- formatNode uses workflow.IsCompleted() to dim completed tickets and
  ui.TypeStyle() for everything else — replaces hardcoded 'done'/'canceled'

cmd/pm/tree_test.go:
- Pass nil workflow to formatNode in unit tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add render hook functionality and vertex buffer pooling (#35)

* Add render hook functionality and vertex buffer pooling

- Introduced GameRenderHook.cpp and GameRenderHook.hpp to implement render hooks.
- Added vertex buffer pooling to optimize buffer management during rendering.
- Updated CMakeLists.txt to include new source files.
- Modified GameOpenGl.cpp to set render hook functions.
- Enhanced GameLuaModule.cpp to expose new Lua functions for enabling vertex buffer pooling.
- Updated game configuration to support vertex buffer pooling option.
- Removed obsolete OpenGL hook implementation.

* Refactor GameRenderHook and gameModConfig to improve memory handling and configuration loading

* Update src/DontStarveInjector/GameRenderHook.cpp

commit.message:
fix: auto-close implemented issues on merge (#72)

## Problem

Generated implement PRs use `Implements #N` in the PR body. GitHub does
not treat `Implements` as an auto-closing keyword, so completed issues
stay open after the PR merges.

## Solution

Switch the generated PR body to `Closes #N` so issue state tracks merged
implementations automatically.

## Changes

- update `implement_issue()` PR body wording from `Implements` to
`Closes`
- add a regression test asserting the generated PR body contains the
auto-closing keyword

Co-authored-by: Colin Cheung <11246116+ColinCee@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add upgrade mode to codenook-init

When re-initializing a project that already has CodeNook:
- Preserve runtime data: task-board.json, memory/, config.json, reviews/
- Regenerate framework files: agent profiles, HITL scripts, engine
- Merge config.json: update version, add new keys, keep user settings
- Skip interactive prompts in upgrade mode (use existing config)
- Show preserved vs updated counts in completion summary

Three choices on existing installation:
  Upgrade (recommended) | Fresh install | Cancel

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: auto-maintained wiki — watcher + maintenance agent + lifecycle hooks (#256-258)

The wiki now self-maintains on every code change:

WikiWatcher (#256):
- FNV-1a file hashing, recursive directory walk
- Extension filtering (.rs .ts .py .go .md .json)
- Ignore patterns (node_modules, .git, target)
- Detects created/modified/deleted files via hash diff

WikiMaintenanceAgent (#257):
- Plans actions per change: create/update/delete pages
- Post-pass: rebuild index.md, run lint, append log.md
- execute_action dispatches each action type

WikiAutoTrigger (#258):
- on_session_start: take initial project snapshot
- on_agent_turn_complete: diff → plan → execute → update snapshot
- on_session_end: final maintenance pass
- force_maintenance: manual trigger
- Snapshot taken AFTER actions to prevent re-detection loops

Flow: edit code → agent turn completes → wiki auto-updates
No manual intervention needed.

Tests: 1015 → 1040 (25 new tests)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

GPM-90: Support multiple --field flags in pm edit

- Change --field from StringVar to StringArrayVar so all occurrences
  are collected by cobra rather than silently keeping only the last one
- Validate all field=value pairs (ParseFieldValue + milestone existence)
  before writing anything to disk — fail-fast, no partial updates
- Apply all field changes in a single read/modify/write pass via the
  new applyTicketFields(ticketPath, []fieldUpdate) helper
- updateTicketField delegates to applyTicketFields for single-field callers
- Success message lists all updated field names, e.g.
  '✓ Updated priority, points for GPM-90'
- All existing tests pass

commit.message:
feat: add -readrate to per-track ffmpeg for near-realtime output

Instead of modifying MA server's -re flag globally (which would break
Spotify Connect and other realtime plugin sources), throttle our own
per-track ffmpeg input reading to ~1.1x with a 5-second initial burst.

This makes our PCM output arrive at near-realtime rate, compatible
with MA's -re flag on the outer ffmpeg, while eliminating startup
latency via the initial burst.

Applied to both _stream_track and prebuffer _fill paths.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Continue backend architecture cleanup with domain-specific partial classes and backend infrastructure splits (#328)

* Initial plan

* refactor: split Query.cs into partial class files by domain

Extract 3 domain sections from the 4219-line Query.cs into separate
partial class files:

- Query.Rankings.cs: Player/company rankings, company management,
  game state (GetRankings, GetCompanyRankings, GetMyCompanies,
  GetCompanyBrands, GetCompanySettings, GetGameState, GetStarterIndustries)

- Query.Building.cs: Building lots, exchange, inventory, analytics,
  ledger, power grid, first-sale mission, media houses, marketing stats

- Query.Lending.cs: Bank lending marketplace (loan offers, loans,
  procurement preview, sourcing candidates, unit upgrade info)

Query.cs retains auth/admin/news/stock-exchange, world data/encyclopedia,
and result type classes. Query.Chat.cs remains unchanged.

No logic changes — pure file reorganization.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: scholtz <1223439+scholtz@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: split Mutation.cs into domain-specific partial class files

Extract building operations, lending, and stock exchange mutations from
the 3455-line Mutation.cs into separate partial class files:

- Mutation.Building.cs (993 lines): StoreBuildingConfiguration,
  CancelBuildingConfiguration, SetBuildingForSale, SetRentPerSqm,
  PurchaseLot, CompleteFirstSaleMilestone, UpdatePublicSalesPrice,
  FlushStorage, ScheduleUnitUpgrade, plus building validation helpers
  and FlushStorageResult/FlushStorageEntry result types.

- Mutation.Lending.cs (397 lines): PublishLoanOffer, UpdateLoanOffer,
  DeactivateLoanOffer, AcceptLoan.

- Mutation.StockExchange.cs (421 lines): BuyShares, SellShares, plus
  share pricing snapshot, trading account resolution, shareholding
  management, and ownership ratio helpers.

- Mutation.cs (1699 lines): Auth, admin, news, company management,
  onboarding, and shared private helpers (GetCurrentTickAsync,
  AddCompanyLedgerEntry, PrepareLotPurchaseAsync, etc.).

All 719 backend tests pass. Pure refactor with no behavioral changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: scholtz <1223439+scholtz@users.noreply.github.com>

* Split large backend GraphQL types into domain-specific partial classes

perf: borrowed-frame for CallMethod (Python method calls)

Extend the borrowed-frame pattern to CallMethod handler. When calling
a simple Python method (is_simple + matching arg count), take the
method object from the stack and borrow its Arc fields via ptr::read
instead of cloning 3 Arcs + 1 builtins Arc.

commit.message:
fix: vitals history + clickable stat tiles + duplicate admission prevention

- Fix vitals not showing in history: JSONB vitals_log returned as JS array
  by pg driver, JSON.parse(array) was silently failing. Added parseJsonbArray()
  helper that handles both array and string forms.
- Stat tiles now clickable — click any tile to filter patient list to that
  status. Click again or click Total Occupied to clear filter. Active tile
  shows filled color + 'Filtered' label (same UX as HospitalWorkflow).
- Duplicate admission prevention: admitPatient() now checks for existing
  active admission before INSERT — returns HTTP 409 with clear message.
- handleVitalsSubmit: added vitalsSubmitting state, ⏳ loading button,
  proper catch block that surfaces error in red banner (was empty catch).
- Applied parseJsonbArray() to medications JSONB field in history modal too.

feat: add AI image generation to recipe image upload (#347)

Adds useAIImageGeneration hook and integrates a '✨ Generate image'
button into the RecipeFormSteps image upload zone.

- useAIImageGeneration hook manages loading/success/error state
- Button disabled when recipe name is empty

- On success: sets generated image as recipe imagePreview

Closes theandiman/recipe-management#41

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: borrowed-frame optimization for simple function calls

Skip Arc::clone/drop for code/globals/builtins/constant_cache when
calling simple functions. Instead, bitwise-copy Arcs from the function
object and keep it alive in held_func. Saves 8 atomic operations per
non-recursive function call.

- Added held_func: Option<PyObjectRef> field to Frame
- Added Frame::new_borrowed() constructor using ptr::read
- Updated recycle() to handle held_func before ManuallyDrop
- Updated CallFunction and LoadGlobalCallFunction handlers
